title: Unchanged variables should be marked as const
description: Unchanged variables should be marked as "const"Analyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEperformanceWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoIf a variable that is not supposed to change is not marked asconst, it could be accidentally reassigned elsewhere in the code, leading to unexpected behavior and bugs that can be hard to track down.By declaring a variable asconst, you ensure that its value remains constant throughout the code. It also signals to other developers that this value is intended to remain constant. This can make the code easier to understand and maintain.In some cases, usingconstcan lead to performance improvements.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Find local variables that are assigned once and never modified

conditions: Variable is declared with initial value
Variable is not reassigned anywhere in scope

never_trigger_on: Reference types that cannot be const
logic.reasoning: Variables that never change should be marked as const for clarity, safety (prevent accidental modification), and potential performance benefits.
